zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Smythe於1525年出生在英國賀特福爾黑費爾德時，她的父親William Smythe26歲，母親Margaret Rose Ingold43歲。她於1548年7月22日與Thomas Kirby在英國賀特福爾黑費爾德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1573年在英國賀特福爾賀特福德享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Smythe于1525年出生在英国賀特福爾黑費爾德时，她的父亲William Smythe26岁，母亲Margaret Rose Ingold43岁。她于1548年7月22日与Thomas Kirby在英国賀特福爾黑費爾德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1573年在英国賀特福爾賀特福德享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: Smythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Smythe was born in 1525, in Therfield, Hertfordshire, England, her father, William Smythe, was 26 and her mother, Margaret Rose Ingold, was 43. She married Thomas Kirby on 22 July 1548, in Therfield, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. She died in 1573, in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Smythe est née en 1525, à Therfield, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, William Smythe, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margaret Rose Ingold, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Kirby le 22 juillet 1548, à Therfield, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée en 1573, à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Smythe 1525 in Therfield, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Smythe 26 und ihre Mutter Margaret Rose Ingold 43. Sie hat am 22. Juli 1548 in Therfield, Hertfordshire, England Thomas Kirby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb 1573 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Smythe è nata il nel 1525 nel Therfield, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, William Smythe, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Margaret Rose Ingold, aveva 43 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Kirby il 22 luglio 1548, nel Therfield, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie. Morì nel 1573, nel Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Smytheが1525年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスエルフィールドで生まれた時，父William Smytheは26歳，母Margaret Rose Ingoldは43歳であった。1548年7月22日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスエルフィールドでThomas Kirbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1573年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォードで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Smythe(이)가 1525년 영국 허트퍼드실르 서르필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Smythe(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Rose Ingold(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 1548년 7월 22일 영국 허트퍼드실르 서르필드에서 Thomas Kirby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1573년 영국 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Smythe nasceu em 1525, em Therfield, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Smythe, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Rose Ingold, tinha 43 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Kirby em 22 de julho de 1548, em Therfield, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1573, em Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Smythe родилась в 1525 г. в местности тирфиелд, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Smythe было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Rose Ingold, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Kirby 22 июля 1548 г. в местности тирфиелд, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла в 1573 г. в местности хиртфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет.
